query: Samsung Electronics HBM3E HBM4 NVIDIA qualification status June 2026

---

# Samsung Electronics HBM3E / HBM4 NVIDIA Qualification Status — June 2026

## Executive Summary

As of early June 2026, Samsung Electronics has **not confirmed passage of NVIDIA's qualification tests for HBM3E**, and **SK Hynix remains NVIDIA's dominant HBM supplier**. Samsung has, however, reportedly secured an **HBM4 supply deal with OpenAI** (not NVIDIA directly), and views HBM4 as a critical "reset opportunity" to close the gap with SK Hynix. The broader memory supercycle — driven by AI accelerator demand — has lifted both companies' valuations dramatically, but SK Hynix's HBM leadership is reflected in its stronger stock performance.

---

## 1. Samsung HBM3E — NVIDIA Qualification

- **Samsung has struggled persistently to qualify its 8-layer and 12-layer HBM3E with NVIDIA**, failing thermal management and reliability tests through 2024 and into 2025–2026.
- **SK Hynix was the first to mass-produce HBM3E** and has been NVIDIA's primary supplier for the H100, H200, and Blackwell-generation GPUs.
- **Micron** serves as a secondary NVIDIA HBM supplier; Samsung is a distant third in the NVIDIA-specific HBM segment.
- Samsung's overall HBM market share was estimated at **~30–35%** as of late 2024, but its share of NVIDIA-bound HBM was significantly lower due to qualification failures.
- No publicly confirmed reports indicate Samsung HBM3E has passed full NVIDIA qualification as of June 2026.

## 2. Samsung HBM4 — Development & Customer Wins

- **Samsung reportedly secured an HBM4 supply deal with OpenAI** (per a South Korean newspaper, ~March 19, 2026), marking a potential breakthrough for Samsung in the next-gen HBM cycle.
- **HBM4 specifications**: 2048-bit interface (doubled from HBM3E's 1024-bit), significantly higher bandwidth, and hybrid bonding integration.
- **Mass production timeline**: Both Samsung and SK Hynix targeted late 2025–2026 for HBM4 mass production.
- **Samsung views HBM4 as a "reset opportunity"** to leapfrog SK Hynix's lead established in HBM3/HBM3E generations.
- **NVIDIA's Vera Rubin architecture** (announced GTC 2026, March 16–19) will require HBM4, creating a key upcoming qualification window.
- Samsung is expanding advanced packaging capacity at its **Pyeongtaek campus** to support HBM4 production.

## 3. Competitive Landscape: Samsung vs. SK Hynix

| Metric | Samsung (005930.KS) | SK Hynix (000660.KS) |
|---|---|---|
| Stock price (June 5, 2026) | 334,000 KRW (–4.98% on day) | ~2,360,000 KRW (as of June 2) |
| 52-week range | 56,900 – 370,000 KRW | N/A (crossed $1T on May 27) |
| HBM market share (est.) | ~30–35% overall | ~50–55% overall |
| NVIDIA HBM qualification | Not confirmed for HBM3E | Primary NVIDIA HBM supplier |
| HBM4 customer wins | OpenAI (reported March 2026) | Expected NVIDIA primary |
| SK Hynix May 2026 return | — | +63.1% |
| SK Hynix market cap | — | ~$1.2 trillion (June 2) |

- **Barclays raised price targets** for both Samsung and SK Hynix on May 31, 2026, citing "continued memory tightness."
- **HBM shortage** is expected to persist **through 2028**, per industry reports.
- **Memory chip prices surged ~60% in Q1 2026** (Omdia data).

## 4. Samsung Foundry & NVIDIA Relationship

- Samsung's foundry market share was **7.2%** ($12.63B revenue) in 2025 vs. TSMC's **69.9%** ($122.54B) — the gap widened to 62.7 percentage points.
- Reports from March 2026 suggested **NVIDIA may shift some inference-chip foundry work to Samsung** (related to Groq's LPU technology), though this is separate from HBM qualification.

## 5. Regulatory Context

- **U.S. BIS revoked Validated End-User (VEU) status** for Samsung China Semiconductor (effective December 31, 2025), requiring individual export licenses — impacting Samsung's China-based memory operations.

## 6. Upcoming Catalysts

- **Jensen Huang Seoul visit (~June 5, 2026)**: NVIDIA's CEO was expected to visit Seoul after Computex Taipei (June 1–4), with meetings expected with SK Group/SK Hynix. Any Samsung meetings would be closely watched for HBM4 qualification signals.
- **HBM4 qualification cycle**: As NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform moves toward production, Samsung's ability to qualify HBM4 will be a critical determinant of its competitive position.

---

## Data Gaps

1. No confirmed public report of Samsung HBM3E passing NVIDIA qualification as of June 2026
2. Samsung's exact HBM-specific revenue and NVIDIA supply share are not publicly disclosed
3. Status of Samsung HBM4 qualification with NVIDIA specifically (vs. OpenAI deal) is unknown
4. Outcome of Jensen Huang's June 2026 Seoul visit regarding Samsung is not yet reported
